title: Avery Dennison (AVY) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Avery Dennison (AVY) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 5.0% FCF growth rate is projected. This aligns with historical performance and accounts for steady materials growth coupled with the higher-margin, faster-growing intelligent labels (RFID) segment offsetting cyclical apparel weakness.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.0% discount rate is utilized, balancing the company's relatively stable cash flows and manageable debt load with its exposure to broader macroeconomic and retail cycles.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.0% terminal growth rate reflects long-term, mature industrial growth slightly trailing global GDP, acknowledging the physical limitations of scaling materials production indefinitely.

thesisParagraphs: Avery Dennison is widely recognized as a stalwart in the traditional pressure-sensitive materials space, often perceived as a slow-growing legacy industrial business. However, this perception overlooks its structural shift toward high-tech, intelligent labeling solutions.

Through its expanding RFID technology portfolio, AVY is embedding itself deeper into global supply chains. As retailers and logistics providers demand higher visibility, item-level tracking, and automated inventory management, AVY's smart labels provide critical infrastructure. This segment offers faster growth and higher margins than its traditional adhesives. While cyclicality in the apparel sector (a key end-market) remains a short-term headwind, the long-term secular trend toward connected packaging and digital IDs underpins a reliable expansion of free cash flow, justifying a measured premium to its historical valuation.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini assign a 5.0% growth rate for Avery Dennison?
faq.answer: While the core materials segment grows at a slower, GDP-like pace, the rapid expansion of its high-margin Intelligent Labels (RFID) business elevates the overall blended growth rate, justifying a 5.0% projection.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for AVY's DCF?
faq.answer: An 8.0% discount rate was applied. It represents Avery Dennison's stable cash generation as an established industrial leader, offset by inherent cyclical risks tied to the consumer retail sector.

faq.question: What is the primary catalyst for Avery Dennison?
faq.answer: The primary growth driver is the broader adoption of RFID and smart label technologies beyond retail apparel, expanding into massive new markets like logistics, food safety, and healthcare supply chains.
